content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration        Executive/Legal                Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                            322-5901                       322-6441

May 17, 1976


Jeffrey J. Pesses
Brundage, Beeson & Pappy
Attorneys at Law
1625 West Olympic Blvd., Suite 805
Los Angeles, CA  90015

                                          Re: Opinion Request
                                              No. 75-060

Dear Mr. Pesses:

     Thank you for your letter of April 22, 1976, requesting an
opinion regarding political activities of a union. Opinion
requests submitted by an organization's representative must
reveal the identity of the organization on whose behalf the
request is submitted. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18320(a)(1).
Because your letter fails to do this, and also because it raises
no substantial question involving an interpretation of the Political
Reform Act, no formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope
the following informal comments will be helpful.

     The Political Reform Act requires organizations to disclose
receipts and disbursements that are made for "political purposes,"
that is, made "for the purpose of influencing or attempting to
influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination
or election of a candidate or candidates, or the qualification
or passage of any measure." Pursuant to this standard, the
union must report the funds received to support political activities
as "contributions," and the funds expended for political activities
as "expenditures" or "contributions." However, the union need
not report receipts and disbursements for activities that are
unrelated to political activities. Regulations defining the
terms "contribution" and "expenditure," 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections
18215, 18225, and a copy of the Commission's Campaign Manual are
enclosed for your guidance.

     If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion request,
you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321. If
you have any additional questions, please contact Natalie West,
an attorney with the Legal Division of the Commission staff.

                                          Sincerely,

                                          [signature]

                                          Michael Bennett
                                          Executive Director

MB:plh

BRUNDAGE, BEESON & PAPI
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
1625 WEST OLYMPIC BOULEVARD, SUITE 605
LOS ANGELES, CALIF. 90015 — (213) 385-5091

Request No. 8 W 7370766

Date Received 4/29/76

Response Due 5/13-/76

FPD 3/8/N'76

BRUNDAGE, BEESON, TAYER & KOVACH
150 BUSH STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, 94104
(415) 658-4030

BRUNDAGE, WILLIAMS & ZELLMAN
3740 FIFTH AVENUE
SAN DIEGO 92103
(714) 297-1131

ROBERT BRUNDAGE
MARSHALL HARDY
ROGER FORHMER
RICHARD J. DAVIS, JR.
STEPHEN H. NAIMAN
WILLIAM J. SMITH

JOSEPH J. KAPLON
JEFFREY J. FISCHEL
ROBERT E. JESSINGER
ROBERT E. WEINER
ROBERT D. VOGEL
MARTIN M. KOGISH

STEVEN ROSEMAN
OF COUNSEL

April 22, 1976

IN REPLY REFER TO:

Fair Political Practices
Commission
1100 "K" Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

This is with reference to a request which we have
received for information by a union on a committee which is registered with the Secretary of State and which proposes to obtain
the following objectives:

"1. To establish a scholarship fund for deserving
   youngsters of our members.

2. To offer discount consumer buying programs
   when and if possible.

3. To participate in other civic organizations.

4. To help our political friends and defeat our
   political enemies.

5. To offer recreational facilities and functions.

6. In general, to do things other than the 'normal'
   type of Union; i.e., process grievances, negotiate contracts, etc.

We propose to raise money in whatever fashion we
can, including raffles, fund raising dinners, etc."

The broad range of activities that are proposed raise
questions for which this office would like an authorized opinion so
as to avoid any misunderstandings between the committee members and
officers and the Fair Political Practices Commission.

In the event you seek further information concerning

[Page number] 51

I can see this is a nearly blank document with only a few faint marks visible at the top of the page. There appear to be two small circular or crescent-shaped marks near the top center, and possibly some very faint marks in the upper right corner, but no clearly legible text is visible to transcribe.

Page Two

the proposed method of raising funds or uses of monies or accounting, please so advise this office and we shall supply you with
that information.

We appreciate your anticipated cooperation.

Very truly yours,

BRUNDAGE BEESON & PAPPY

By
Jeffrey J. Pesses

JJP:cjc
cc: John Matull
